title: Misato and her change of heart between 222 and 333
display_title: Misato and her change of heart between 222 and 333
wikitext:
[]: Misato and her change of heart between 2.22 and 3.33             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Tue Oct 14, 2014 4:33 pm **If there's already a thread on this please feel free to direct me to it! ** So this is something that's been bothering me ever since I watched 3.0 several months back. Between 2.22 and 3.0 (3.33) Misato seems to do a complete 360 on her opinion of Shinji. She from cheering him on in rescuing Rei II from Zeruel, to possibly doubting the situation (at the very end of 2.22) to being outright short and rude with him in 3.0. The events at the conclusion of 2.22 happened in a fit of rage, and past a certain point were out of his control, but it seems that Misato is putting all of the blame on him. She KNEW he was being used by Gendo, but is a jerk to Shinji anyway. Since she knows how he is after living with him, does anyone have any speculation on why she would act like this? It seems to me that she would, based on her character, nicer and more understanding to Shinji, instead of blaming him for what are essentially the acts of his father.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Oct 14, 2014 4:43 pm My take on it is laid out in my fanfic, starting at Scene 18 . You can also read the notes I made when preparing to write those scenes. Other threads: [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/16661/Misatos-Characterization-Level-EEE/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/16220/Misatos-feelings-towards-Shinji-at-the-start-of-Q/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13412/Misatos-relationship-with-Shinji-in-30/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/14708/Misatos-whisper-of-Shinji-kun-on-the-Wunder/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/14076/Blaming-Misato/[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Tue Oct 14, 2014 4:45 pm Since she knows how he is after living with him, does anyone have any speculation on why she would act like this?

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Tue Oct 14, 2014 4:51 pm pwhodges wrote: My take on it is laid out in my fanfic, starting at Scene 18 . You can also read the notes I made when preparing to write those scenes. Other threads: [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/16661/Misatos-Characterization-Level-EEE/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/16220/Misatos-feelings-towards-Shinji-at-the-start-of-Q/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13412/Misatos-relationship-with-Shinji-in-30/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/14708/Misatos-whisper-of-Shinji-kun-on-the-Wunder/[/url] [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/14076/Blaming-Misato/[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Tue Oct 14, 2014 5:07 pm 14 years of hell and war caused (either directly or indirectly) by the closest thing you ever had to a son will turn anyone into a cold and bitter person.

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Tue Oct 14, 2014 5:13 pm But that's like blaming a puppet for murder when the puppetmaster still has the strings in his hands. And on TOP of that, Seele is STILL even higher on that train then Gendo, since Seele is who stopped 3I and sealed Shinji and Rei II inside of Unit 01.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Tue Oct 14, 2014 5:19 pm It's because she hasn't had a beer in 14 years.

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Tue Oct 14, 2014 5:27 pm Guy Nacks wrote: It's because she hasn't had a beer in 14 years.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Tue Oct 14, 2014 5:57 pm Ultrahuntr wrote: See, this is something more believable. Haha.

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Tue Oct 14, 2014 6:20 pm unitM wrote: It's because she hasn't seen Kaji in 14(+?) years. hint hint

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Oct 15, 2014 2:55 am Well, the problem is that we didn't really get to see that much of Misato, the only moment of characterization was her reaction to the surprise return of Shinji, that no one foresaw or was prepared for. To really see if she had a change of heart, we need to see her in a quieter situation without the stress of a NS attack or an Impact underway, where she have the time to think about Shinji's return and how she feels about it. Since Gendo will probably need some time to prepare for his plan (he wouldn't be building an army of MP-Evas if that wasn't the case), I'm sure that we'll know more of her feelings (and the rest of the WILLE staff) in Shin Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Wed Oct 15, 2014 11:01 am That's agreeable. Plus we do see a tinge of her old self when she's holding up the remote for the choker at Shinji but can't bring herself to kill Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Oct 15, 2014 12:32 pm Ultrahuntr wrote: That's agreeable. Plus we do see a tinge of her old self when she's holding up the remote for the choker at Shinji but can't bring herself to kill Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Wed Oct 15, 2014 1:09 pm Good point. At that, I'm really interested to see where her character development goes in Final.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Wed Oct 15, 2014 1:13 pm Pwhodges seems to have a good idea of a possible development, which I'm 100% behind, in my opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by Ultrahuntr » Wed Oct 15, 2014 4:01 pm I actually like it a lot too. It seems pretty strong as far as possibilities go.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Oct 15, 2014 5:21 pm I believe I have probably made a pretty close stab at Misato's thoughts and feelings in Q - but how Anno will actually develop them in the last film is of course something I'm not privy to; but he surely won't make her feelings any less sympathetic. Anyway, beyond that my intention is to stay, as strictly as I can manage, faithful to existing canon as I read it, and to provide a plausible continuation on those terms with a sensible amount of character development. However, I can't pretend that my four months of intermittent amateur thought about this will compare in any way with Anno's ten or twenty years living it. My Shinji has probably had it too easy, if what he has had to go through for his final mental growth previously is anything to go by; in particular, I expect the last film to include something at least comparable with the earlier instrumentality, which in my story will be averted.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Oct 15, 2014 6:15 pm pwhodges wrote: My Shinji has probably had it too easy, if what he has had to go through for his final mental growth previously is anything to go by; in particular, I expect the last film to include something at least comparable with the earlier instrumentality, which in my story will be averted.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Wed Oct 15, 2014 6:34 pm He didn't reached "fap to comatose gir" level yet, so no. He can be broken some more

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Oct 15, 2014 6:46 pm ^ No, he already reached the state "broken unresponsive sack of meat" that comes after the fapping in EoE, and without even needing it this time! Seriously I just don't see how he could be broken even more, even his catatonic self in EoE still had some presence to answer to Misato from time to time. The one at the end of Q could be considered dead ! There's nothing else to broke, only to recover, and breaking him again after that while there's still one movie left is really less than likely.
